text: Walked through the lexintel plugin's pipeline engine: first tracing every invocation path for engine/compile_step.py (concluding there are exactly two plus a non-path â€” the automatic one being the run_pipeline.py runner calling compile_step.compile_contract() in-process mid-run when it reaches a step whose contract is stale). Then built a shareable visual guide artifact from a fresh read of all six engine modules plus _runlib.py and reference/model.md, laying out the engine/instance split and its four crossing seams, the spec_lib dataclass and on-disk JSON schemas, component relationship/import/subprocess edge tables (including the only four LLM call sites), and vertical sequence diagrams of the run loop's six gates, the compile gate, per-mode step lanes, and status derivation. Finally kicked off a pipeline run on the HOTEL workspace: archived the completed prior state into pipeline/workspaces/HOTEL/archive/ (41 files) and launched a background --stale repair run covering A1 (edited spec) plus its 38 downstream steps to regenerate memo.md, watchable live on the dashboard at http://127.0.0.1:8191. The final background task ended in a killed state.
